source.notes: Bug shape: in playthrough 5 the player picked a quick action tagged [Persuasion or Intimidation] on turn 4, but the Narrator did not surface a request_roll — it narrated the social pressure to resolution without rolling. Earlier turns had rolled correctly. Root cause: the Narrator's roll-frequency heuristic ('one check every 2-3 turns') silently overrode explicit player intent. Fix: programmatic detection of [Skill] tags in playerInput injects a binding directive into the per-turn delta. This fixture asserts the directive is present when a tag is detected, and that non-rollable bracket text ('[No roll — a pure choice]') does NOT trigger it.

turns.narratorProse: Opening prose for an established scene with prior rolls already surfaced.

input.playerInput: Turn to Kess and demand the truth about the missing Resonant. [Persuasion or Intimidation]

narrator.prose: (unused — fixture asserts on buildMessagesForNarrator output, not narrator response)

narratorMessages.playerInput: Turn to Kess and demand the truth about the missing Resonant. [Persuasion or Intimidation]
